title: Razorpay Reverse Transaction Chain
description: Razorpay maintains each merchant's transactions as a singly linked list ordered chronologically. For an end-of-day report they need the chain reversed (latest first). Given the head of a list (encoded as an array of node values), return the values of the reversed list.
hints: Three pointers: prev, curr, next — classic in-place reverse.
Loop: save next, flip curr.next to prev, advance prev/curr.
Edge case: empty list — return null directly.

title: Swiggy Detect Delivery Route Loop
description: Swiggy chains delivery hops as a linked list. A misconfigured route can loop forever. To represent this in tests, we use the array `[values, pos]` where `pos` is the 0-based index of the node that the tail's `next` points back to (or `-1` if no cycle). Return `true` if the list has a cycle, else `false`.
constraints: pos is -1 or a valid index
hints: Floyd's tortoise + hare: slow moves 1, fast moves 2.
Agar fast slow se mil gaya → cycle. Agar fast null hua → no cycle.
Edge: empty or single node with pos=-1 → false.

title: Flipkart Middle Wishlist Item
description: Flipkart shows users the 'middle' item of their wishlist as a featured suggestion. Given the wishlist as a linked list, return the value of the middle node. If there are two middle nodes (even length), return the second middle.
hints: Slow + fast pointer trick — slow ek step, fast do step.
Jab fast null/last hit kare, slow middle pe hoga.
Even length me slow second middle pe hi rukta hai if you advance slow whenever fast & fast.next exist.

title: PhonePe Remove Nth Recent Payment
description: PhonePe lets users undo their `n`-th most recent payment. Payments form a linked list with the oldest at the head and newest at the tail. Given the head and `n`, remove the n-th node counting from the end (1-indexed) and return the resulting list values.
hints: Dummy node use kar — head removal handle ho jayegi.
Two pointer: fast ko n+1 ahead bhej, fir dono ko saath chala.
Jab fast null hit kare, slow.next is the node to skip.

title: Paytm Merge Sorted UPI Queues
description: Paytm's UPI gateway has two priority queues represented as sorted linked lists (ascending by priority). Merge them into one sorted linked list and return its values. Both inputs are sorted; the result must also be sorted ascending.
hints: Dummy node + tail pointer build pattern.
Two pointers a, b — pick smaller, advance.
Loop end ke baad jo bacha use append kar de.

title: Ola Remove Duplicate Trip Logs
description: Ola's trip log is a sorted linked list of trip distances. Some trips were logged twice. Remove duplicates so each distance appears only once. The list is already sorted. Return the resulting values.
hints: Single pointer walk — agar curr.val == curr.next.val toh skip.
Else move forward.
Empty / single list → return as-is.

title: CRED Palindrome Card History
description: CRED treats certain credit-card last-4-digit patterns as 'lucky' if their history sequence reads the same forward and backward. Given the linked list of card history values, return `true` if it's a palindrome.
hints: Find middle, reverse second half, compare with first half.
Or simpler: dump values into array and two-pointer compare.
Empty / single → trivially palindrome.

title: Zomato Reverse Between Bookmarks
description: Zomato lets users bookmark a range of past orders. Given a linked list of order IDs and 1-indexed positions `left` and `right`, reverse the nodes from position `left` to `right` (inclusive) in place and return the resulting list values.
hints: Dummy node se start kar — head reverse ho sakta hai.
Walk to node before `left`, then in-place pointer reverse for (right - left) iterations.
Splice carefully: pre-left.next ko naya head, original-left.next ko remainder.

title: Uber Rotate Driver Shifts
description: Uber rotates a daily driver-shift list to the right by `k` positions every morning. Given the linked list and `k`, return the values after rotation. `k` may exceed the length.
hints: k % length kar — bada k handle ho jata hai.
List ko circle banao (tail.next = head), fir new tail dhoondh.
New head = node after new tail; break the cycle.

title: TCS Odd Even Employee Reorder
description: TCS reorders an employee linked list so that all nodes at odd positions (1-indexed) come first, followed by nodes at even positions, while preserving relative order within each group. Do it in O(1) extra space (rearrange pointers).
hints: Maintain two chains: odd tail and even tail.
Iterate: odd.next = odd.next.next, even.next = even.next.next.
Finally connect odd-tail to even-head.

title: Wipro Add Two Bill Numbers
description: Wipro stores giant bill amounts as linked lists where each node holds a digit, with the LEAST significant digit at the head (so 342 is stored as 2 → 4 → 3). Given two such lists, return the sum as a linked list in the same format.
constraints: No leading zeros except the number 0 itself
hints: Iterate dono lists, ek digit-by-digit add carry ke saath.
Carry next iteration me forward kar, end me agar carry > 0 toh extra node.
Different lengths handle: jo khatam hua usko 0 treat kar.

title: Meesho Swap Adjacent Pair Products
description: Meesho's merchandising team A/B-tests by swapping adjacent product pairs in a feed. Given the linked list, swap every two adjacent nodes (modify pointers, not values) and return the new list values. If a final lone node remains, leave it alone.
hints: Dummy node se start kar.
Maintain prev — har step me ek pair fetch karke pointers shuffle kar.
Recursion bhi clean hai but iterative O(1) space.

title: Razorpay Partition by Payment Threshold
description: Razorpay's risk engine partitions a transaction list by amount: every transaction with amount strictly less than threshold `x` should appear before every transaction with amount >= `x`. Within each side, preserve original relative order.
hints: Build do separate lists: less and greaterEqual, dummy heads ke saath.
Iterate original, append nodes accordingly.
Last me less.tail.next = greaterEqual.head, greaterEqual.tail.next = null.

title: Swiggy Reorder Delivery Feed
description: Swiggy reorders a delivery feed list `L0 → L1 → ... → Ln-1` into `L0 → Ln-1 → L1 → Ln-2 → L2 → ...` to alternate between top and bottom of the list. Modify in place and return the resulting values.
hints: Steps: find middle, reverse second half, then merge two halves alternately.
After reverse, first half ends right before middle.
Be careful: split list into two halves cleanly (first half tail.next = null).

title: Ola Cycle Entry Point
description: Ola's hop chain has a configuration bug that causes a cycle in the route. Given the list (encoded as `arr` and `pos` like the cycle-detection problem), return the VALUE of the node where the cycle begins, or `null` if there is no cycle.
hints: Phase 1: tortoise+hare to find a meeting point inside the cycle.
Phase 2: reset slow to head; advance both 1 step — they meet at entry.
Math: distance from head to entry = distance from meeting to entry (mod cycle).

title: PhonePe Merge K Payment Streams
description: PhonePe receives `k` ascending sorted payment streams (each a linked list of timestamps as integers). Merge them into one ascending sorted list. Input is encoded as an array of arrays.
hints: Pairwise merge: O(N log k).
Or: min-heap of k heads, pop smallest, push its next.
Single dummy + tail builder for the result.

title: Flipkart Reverse Catalog in Groups of K
description: Flipkart's catalog API supports a 'flip-by-page' feature: given the linked list and a page size `k`, reverse every consecutive group of `k` nodes in place. If the last group has fewer than `k` nodes, leave it as-is. Return the resulting values.
hints: Walk in chunks of k. For each chunk, peek ahead to see if k nodes remain.
If yes, reverse those k nodes and re-link with prev tail.
If no, stop. Track group's previous tail to splice.

title: Amazon Sort Fulfillment List
description: Amazon's fulfillment center receives an unsorted linked list of order priorities and needs it sorted ascending. Achieve O(n log n) time and O(1) extra space (excluding recursion/iterative bookkeeping). Return the sorted list values.
hints: Merge sort fits linked lists naturally — split, sort halves, merge.
Iterative bottom-up version achieves O(1) extra space (no recursion stack).
Helper: split head into two halves using slow/fast.
